description: Write operation benchmarks for notion-cli (CLI) vs Notion MCP server. Tests page creation, database creation, task management, and bulk operations.

evals.name: Page Creation
evals.prompt: Create a new Notion page called 'Benchmark Test Page' with this content: a heading 'Overview', a paragraph explaining this is a benchmark test, and a bullet list with 3 items: 'Speed', 'Accuracy', 'Reliability'. Return the page URL when done.
assertions: returns a URL or page ID

evals.name: Page Update
evals.prompt: Find the page called 'Benchmark Test Page' in my Notion workspace. Add a new section at the bottom called 'Results' with a paragraph saying 'All benchmarks passed successfully.' Confirm the update.

evals.name: Database Creation
evals.prompt: Create a Notion database called 'Benchmark Tasks' with these columns: Name (title), Status (select with options: To Do, In Progress, Done), Priority (number), Due Date (date). Return the database URL when done.
assertions: has Status column with select options
returns a URL or database ID

evals.name: Database Row Creation
evals.prompt: Find the 'Benchmark Tasks' database and add these 3 tasks: 1) 'Fix login bug' with Status 'To Do' and Priority 1, 2) 'Update docs' with Status 'In Progress' and Priority 2, 3) 'Add tests' with Status 'To Do' and Priority 3. Confirm all 3 were created.
assertions: each row has correct name and status

evals.name: Task Status Workflow
evals.prompt: Find the task 'Fix login bug' in the 'Benchmark Tasks' database. Update its Status to 'In Progress'. Then update it again to 'Done'. Confirm the final status.

evals.name: Structured Page Creation
evals.prompt: Create a Notion page called 'Architecture Overview' with this structure: heading 'System Components' with 3 sub-items (API Server, Database, Worker Queue), heading 'Data Flow' with a paragraph describing request flow, and heading 'Deployment' with a callout block saying 'Deployed on AWS'. Return the URL.

cleanup_prompt: Search my Notion workspace for pages named 'Benchmark Test Page' or 'Architecture Overview' and databases named 'Benchmark Tasks'. Archive/trash all of them. Confirm what was cleaned up.
with_skill_instructions: You have notion-cli installed. Use `notion-cli workspace search <query>` for lookups, `notion-cli workspace get <query>` to search + fetch content, `notion-cli pages create --parent-page-id <id> --title "Title" --markdown "# Content" --json` to create pages with markdown, `notion-cli db create --parent-page-id <id> --title "Name" --schema "Name TITLE, Status SELECT('To Do','Done')" --json` to create databases with DDL shorthand, `notion-cli pages update <id> --properties '<json>' --json` to update. The Support Docs page ID is 3262dbd5-708f-812f-9073-d2fda63adcde. Test database: Matts Work Board (2f72dbd5-708f).
without_skill_instructions: You have the Notion MCP server connected. Use notion-search to find pages, notion-create-pages to create pages, notion-create-database to create databases, notion-update-page to update pages. The Support Docs page ID is 3262dbd5-708f-812f-9073-d2fda63adcde.
